briefdescription.para: zsock_poll: Poll for readability
zsock_poll: Poll for exceptional condition
zsock_poll: Poll for writability
zsock_poll: Poll results in error condition (output value only)
zsock_poll: Poll detected closed connection (output value only)
zsock_poll: Invalid socket (output value only)

sectiondef.header: Options for sending and receiving data

briefdescription.para: zsock_recv: Read data without removing it from socket input queue
zsock_recvmsg: Control data buffer too small.
zsock_recv: return the real length of the datagram, even when it was longer than the passed buffer
zsock_recv/zsock_send: Override operation to non-blocking
zsock_recv: block until the full amount of data can be returned
zsock_shutdown: Shut down for reading
zsock_shutdown: Shut down for writing
zsock_shutdown: Shut down for both reading and writing
Address for bind() (vs for connect())
Fill in ai_canonname.
Assume host address is in numeric notation, don't DNS lookup.
May return IPv4 mapped address for IPv6
May return both native IPv6 and mapped IPv4 address for IPv6.
IPv4/IPv6 support depends on local system config.
Assume service (port) is numeric.
Extra flags present (see RFC 5014)
zsock_getnameinfo(): Resolve to numeric address.
zsock_getnameinfo(): Resolve to numeric port number.
zsock_getnameinfo(): Return only hostname instead of FQDN
zsock_getnameinfo(): Dummy option for compatibility
zsock_getnameinfo(): Dummy option for compatibility
zsock_getnameinfo(): Max supported hostname length
Socket-level option.
Recording debugging information (ignored, for compatibility)
address reuse
Type of the socket.
Async error.
Bypass normal routing and send directly to host (ignored, for compatibility)
Transmission of broadcast messages is supported (ignored, for compatibility)
Size of socket send buffer.
Size of socket recv buffer.
Enable sending keep-alive messages on connections.
Place out-of-band data into receive stream (ignored, for compatibility)
Socket priority.
Socket lingers on close (ignored, for compatibility)
Allow multiple sockets to reuse a single port.
Receive low watermark (ignored, for compatibility)
Send low watermark (ignored, for compatibility)
Receive timeout Applies to receive functions like recv(), but not to connect()
Send timeout.
Bind a socket to an interface.
Socket accepts incoming connections (ignored, for compatibility)
Timestamp TX RX or both packets.

detaileddescription.para: Supports multiple timestamp sources.

briefdescription.para: Protocol used with the socket.
Domain used with SOCKET.
Enable SOCKS5 for Socket.
Socket TX time (when the data should be sent)
Socket TX time (same as SO_TXTIME)
Timestamp generation flags.

detaileddescription.para: Request RX timestamps generated by network adapter.

briefdescription.para: Request TX timestamps generated by network adapter.

detaileddescription.para: This can be enabled via socket option or control messages.

briefdescription.para: Disable TCP buffering (ignored, for compatibility)
Start keepalives after this period (seconds)
Interval between keepalives (seconds)
Number of keepalives before dropping connection.
Set or receive the Type-Of-Service value for an outgoing packet.
Set or receive the Time-To-Live value for an outgoing packet.
Pass an IP_PKTINFO ancillary message that contains a pktinfo structure that supplies some information about the incoming packet.
Pass an IP_RECVTTL ancillary message that contains information about the time to live of the incoming packet.
Retrieve the current known path MTU of the current socket.

detaileddescription.para: Returns an integer. ZSOCK_IP_MTU is valid only for getsockopt and can be employed only when the socket has been connected.

briefdescription.para: Set IPv4 multicast datagram network interface.
Set IPv4 multicast TTL value.
Set IPv4 multicast loop value.
Join IPv4 multicast group.
Leave IPv4 multicast group.
Clamp down the global port range for a given socket.
Set the unicast hop limit for the socket.
Set multicast output network interface index for the socket.
Set the multicast hop limit for the socket.
Set the multicast loop bit for the socket.
Join IPv6 multicast group.
Leave IPv6 multicast group.
Join IPv6 multicast group.
Leave IPv6 multicast group.
For getsockopt(), retrieve the current known IPv6 path MTU of the given socket.

detaileddescription.para: Valid only when the socket has been connected. For setsockopt(), set the MTU to be used for the socket. The MTU is limited by the device MTU or the path MTU when path MTU discovery is enabled.

briefdescription.para: Don't support IPv4 access.
Pass an IPV6_RECVPKTINFO ancillary message that contains a in6_pktinfo structure that supplies some information about the incoming packet.

detaileddescription.para: See RFC 3542.

briefdescription.para: Option which returns an in6_pktinfo structure in ancillary data.
Pass an IPV6_RECVHOPLIMIT ancillary message that contains information about the hop limit of the incoming packet.

detaileddescription.para: See RFC 3542.

briefdescription.para: Set or receive the hoplimit value for an outgoing packet.
RFC5014: Source address selection.
Prefer temporary address as source.
Prefer public address as source.
Either public or temporary address is selected as a default source depending on the output interface configuration (this is the default value).

detaileddescription.para: This is Linux specific option not found in the RFC.

briefdescription.para: Prefer Care-of address as source.

detaileddescription.para: Ignored in Zephyr.

briefdescription.para: Prefer Home address as source.

detaileddescription.para: Ignored in Zephyr.

briefdescription.para: Prefer CGA (Cryptographically Generated Address) address as source.

detaileddescription.para: Ignored in Zephyr.

briefdescription.para: Prefer non-CGA address as source.

detaileddescription.para: Ignored in Zephyr.

briefdescription.para: Set or receive the traffic class value for an outgoing packet.
listen: The maximum backlog queue length

sectiondef.header: Macros for checking special IPv6 addresses.

briefdescription.para: Check unspecified IPv6 address.
Check loopback IPv6 address.
Check IPv6 multicast address.
Check IPv6 link local address.
Check IPv6 site local address.
Check IPv6 v4 mapped address.
Check IPv6 multicast global address.
Check IPv6 multicast node local address.
Check IPv6 multicast link local address.
Check IPv6 multicast site local address.
Check IPv6 multicast organization local address.
Socket file descriptor set.
Obtain a file descriptor's associated net context.

detaileddescription.para: With CONFIG_USERSPACE enabled, the kernel's object permission system must apply to socket file descriptors. When a socket is opened, by default only the caller has permission, access by other threads will fail unless they have been specifically granted permission.

This is achieved by tagging data structure definitions that implement the underlying object associated with a network socket file descriptor with __net_socket. All pointers to instances of these will be known to the kernel as kernel objects with type K_OBJ_NET_SOCKET.

This API is intended for threads that need to grant access to the object associated with a particular file descriptor to another thread. The returned pointer represents the underlying K_OBJ_NET_SOCKET and may be passed to APIs like k_object_access_grant().

In a system like Linux which has the notion of threads running in processes in a shared virtual address space, this sort of management is unnecessary as the scope of file descriptors is implemented at the process level.

However in Zephyr the file descriptor scope is global, and MPU-based systems are not able to implement a process-like model due to the lack of memory virtualization hardware. They use discrete object permissions and memory domains instead to define thread access scope.

User threads will have no direct access to the returned object and will fault if they try to access its memory; the pointer can only be used to make permission assignment calls, which follow exactly the rules for other kernel objects like device drivers and IPC.

simplesect.para: pointer to associated network socket object, or NULL if the file descriptor wasn't valid or the caller had no access permission

briefdescription.para: Create a network socket.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/socket.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as socket() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

If CONFIG_USERSPACE is enabled, the caller will be granted access to the context object associated with the returned file descriptor.
zsock_get_context_object()

memberdef.argsstring: (int family, int type, int proto, int *sv)
memberdef.name: zsock_socketpair

briefdescription.para: Create an unnamed pair of connected sockets.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/009695399/functions/socketpair.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as socketpair() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Close a network socket.

detaileddescription.para: Close a network socket. This function is also exposed as close() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined (in which case it may conflict with generic POSIX close() function).

briefdescription.para: Shutdown socket send/receive operations.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/shutdown.html for normative description, but currently this function has no effect in Zephyr and provided solely for compatibility with existing code. This function is also exposed as shutdown() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, const struct net_sockaddr *addr, net_socklen_t addrlen)
memberdef.name: zsock_bind

briefdescription.para: Bind a socket to a local network address.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/bind.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as bind() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, const struct net_sockaddr *addr, net_socklen_t addrlen)
memberdef.name: zsock_connect

briefdescription.para: Connect a socket to a peer network address.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/connect.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as connect() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Set up a STREAM socket to accept peer connections.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/listen.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as listen() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Accept a connection on listening socket.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/accept.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as accept() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, const void *buf, size_t len, int flags, const struct net_sockaddr *dest_addr, net_socklen_t addrlen)
memberdef.name: zsock_sendto

briefdescription.para: Send data to an arbitrary network address.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/sendto.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as sendto() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, const void *buf, size_t len, int flags)
memberdef.name: zsock_send

briefdescription.para: Send data to a connected peer.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/send.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as send() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, const struct net_msghdr *msg, int flags)
memberdef.name: zsock_sendmsg

briefdescription.para: Send data to an arbitrary network address.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/sendmsg.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as sendmsg() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, void *buf, size_t max_len, int flags, struct net_sockaddr *src_addr, net_socklen_t *addrlen)
memberdef.name: zsock_recvfrom

briefdescription.para: Receive data from an arbitrary network address.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/recvfrom.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as recvfrom() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Receive a message from an arbitrary network address.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/recvmsg.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as recvmsg() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, void *buf, size_t max_len, int flags)
memberdef.name: zsock_recv

briefdescription.para: Receive data from a connected peer.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/recv.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as recv() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Control blocking/non-blocking mode of a socket.

detaileddescription.para: This functions allow to (only) configure a socket for blocking or non-blocking operation (O_NONBLOCK). This function is also exposed as fcntl() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined (in which case it may conflict with generic POSIX fcntl() function).

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, unsigned long request, va_list ap)
memberdef.name: zsock_ioctl_impl

briefdescription.para: Control underlying socket parameters.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/ioctl.html for normative description. This function enables querying or manipulating underlying socket parameters. Currently supported request values include ZFD_IOCTL_FIONBIO, and ZFD_IOCTL_FIONREAD, to set non-blocking mode, and query the number of bytes available to read, respectively. This function is also exposed as ioctl() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined (in which case it may conflict with generic POSIX ioctl() function).

briefdescription.para: Efficiently poll multiple sockets for events.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/poll.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as poll() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined (in which case it may conflict with generic POSIX poll() function).

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, int level, int optname, void *optval, net_socklen_t *optlen)
memberdef.name: zsock_getsockopt

briefdescription.para: Get various socket options.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/getsockopt.html for normative description. In Zephyr this function supports a subset of socket options described by POSIX, but also some additional options available in Linux (some options are dummy and provided to ease porting of existing code). This function is also exposed as getsockopt() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, int level, int optname, const void *optval, net_socklen_t optlen)
memberdef.name: zsock_setsockopt

briefdescription.para: Set various socket options.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/setsockopt.html for normative description. In Zephyr this function supports a subset of socket options described by POSIX, but also some additional options available in Linux (some options are dummy and provided to ease porting of existing code). This function is also exposed as setsockopt() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Get peer name.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/getpeername.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as getpeername() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Get socket name.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/getsockname.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as getsockname() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Get local host name.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/gethostname.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as gethostname() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (net_sa_family_t family, const void *src, char *dst, size_t size)
memberdef.name: zsock_inet_ntop

briefdescription.para: Convert network address from internal to numeric ASCII form.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/inet_ntop.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as inet_ntop() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Convert network address from numeric ASCII form to internal representation.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/inet_pton.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as inet_pton() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *host, const char *service, const struct zsock_addrinfo *hints, struct zsock_addrinfo **res)
memberdef.name: zsock_getaddrinfo

briefdescription.para: Resolve a domain name to one or more network addresses.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/getaddrinfo.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as getaddrinfo() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Free results returned by zsock_getaddrinfo()

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/freeaddrinfo.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as freeaddrinfo() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Convert zsock_getaddrinfo() error code to textual message.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/gai_strerror.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as gai_strerror() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct net_sockaddr *addr, net_socklen_t addrlen, char *host, net_socklen_t hostlen, char *serv, net_socklen_t servlen, int flags)
memberdef.name: zsock_getnameinfo

briefdescription.para: Resolve a network address to a domain name or ASCII address.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/getnameinfo.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as getnameinfo() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, const void *buf, size_t len, int flags, k_timeout_t timeout, size_t *sent_len)
memberdef.name: zsock_send_all

briefdescription.para: Send data to an arbitrary network address.

detaileddescription.para: The function will try to send all the data requested, blocking if necessary. User can set a limit on the time spent in the function using socket send timeout.

See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/sendto.html for normative description of the parameters. The function differs from zsock_send() by trying to send all the data requested, blocking if necessary until all data is sent or timeout occurs. Note that this is only applicable for stream sockets like TCP. If the timeout is set to K_NO_WAIT, the function will return after the first send attempt, possibly sending less data than requested.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to data buffer to send
Maximum time to wait until data is sent
Pointer to variable where to store the actual number of bytes sent. Can be set to NULL if the caller does not need this information.

memberdef.argsstring: (int sock, const struct net_msghdr *msg, int flags, k_timeout_t timeout, size_t *sent_len)
memberdef.name: zsock_sendmsg_all

briefdescription.para: Send data to an arbitrary network address.

detaileddescription.para: The function will try to send all the data requested, blocking if necessary. User can set a limit on the time spent in the function using socket send timeout.

See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/sendmsg.html for normative description of the parameters. The function differs from zsock_sendmsg() by trying to send all the data requested, blocking if necessary until all data is sent or timeout occurs. Note that this is only applicable for stream sockets like TCP. If the timeout is set to K_NO_WAIT, the function will return after the first send attempt, possibly sending less data than requested.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to message header describing data to send
Maximum time to wait until data is sent
Pointer to variable where to store the actual number of bytes sent. Can be set to NULL if the caller does not need this information.

memberdef.argsstring: (int nfds, zsock_fd_set *readfds, zsock_fd_set *writefds, zsock_fd_set *exceptfds, struct zsock_timeval *timeout)
memberdef.name: zsock_select

briefdescription.para: Legacy function to poll multiple sockets for events.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/select.html for normative description. This function is provided to ease porting of existing code and not recommended for usage due to its inefficiency, use zsock_poll() instead. In Zephyr this function works only with sockets, not arbitrary file descriptors. This function is also exposed as select() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined (in which case it may conflict with generic POSIX select() function).

briefdescription.para: Initialize (clear) fd_set.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/select.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as FD_ZERO() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Check whether socket is a member of fd_set.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/select.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as FD_ISSET() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Remove socket from fd_set.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/select.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as FD_CLR() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Add socket to fd_set.

detaileddescription.para: See POSIX.1-2017 article http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/functions/select.html for normative description. This function is also exposed as FD_SET() if CONFIG_POSIX_API is defined.

briefdescription.para: Number of file descriptors which can be added to zsock_fd_set.
BSD Sockets compatible API.
